The 5Cs of a Successful Conversion Coach (Completed 05/07/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 1 of 5
Speaker 1: All right, so a very very warm welcome back to the final lesson of module one. 5C's of a
successful conversion coach. Now let me start off by saying that one of the goals of this
program is to really arm you with the tools to accelerate the process of building trust
and intimacy with your audience. And doing so at scale in a way that makes it easier for
you to facilitate or coach the conversion. And this applies to online entrepreneurs
speaking to their own audience as well, and maybe even especially for copywriters and
freelancers who are doing this for their clients. Because there's this myth. You don't
need years to develop that relationship and bond. In fact I want you to take a second
right now to think about someone who came into your life, you know seemingly out of
nowhere. And maybe after one exchange, one drink, one fleeting moment, you felt an
instant connection. You know that's what we're trying to achieve here.
Now if you're wondering what this has to do with copywriting and online product
launches and funnels, the short answer is everything. You know a sale is a
transformation. And unless you've earned the right to influence that transformation, no
copywriting trick in the world will help you get the conversion. So if you'll stay with me
here, I want to do a quick two minute exercise before we dive into the main part of this
lesson.
So I want you to close your eyes and really think about the person you feel most at ease
with. Most comfortable. So someone you really, really trust. You know someone whose
advice you're most open to taking. You know most willing to explore and embrace what
they have to offer. Which is pretty much just another way of saying they're influential to
you. And once you have this person in your mind, I want you to take just a few extra
moments to tap into why this person is so influential and important to you. Really ask
what is it about them that makes you feel so good? So at ease? And so ready to
transform in their presence? What are those qualities? And if you feel compelled to do
so, I fully invite you to take a second to pause this video right now and write down, just
sketch out anything that comes to mind. Anything that comes to mind about why you
feel so at ease with this person. Why you are so ready to embrace what they have to say
and act on what they have to say. So take just a few seconds to pause this video and
write out anything that comes to mind, and then we'll go a little deeper on this in just a
few seconds.
All right. So hopefully you took a moment to explore. You know if this was a live
workshop, this is where I'd love to hear what was going through your mind. But for now,
I do want to offer some possible insights that may have come up for you. And just see
how that matches with some of the insights you generated. You know a lot of the times
when I do this exercise in a live workshop people are like super astonished to realize
that the person that came up for them wasn't necessarily a parent, a partner, or a life
long friend. In fact, almost paradoxically, people resist the influence of the people who
they know best. Which sounds kind of strange, right? Like why might you be more
influence-able by a person who just swiped right on a dating app than the person who
swiped the dirt off your face when you were young?
And here's why. Here's my thesis around that. It's that we're influenced not by the ones
we know best, but the ones who make the best effort to know us. You know these are
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 5 --

The 5Cs of a Successful Conversion Coach (Completed 05/07/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 2 of 5
the people who ask us questions and actually listen. Actually listen to what we have to
say instead of just assuming they know us. You know they're present with our hopes,
our fears, our dreams. And in the best cases, you know they're present with the
underlying or unconscious factors around them. You know they're the ones who see
things that we haven't even seen for ourselves and they can reflect it back to us. So not
only do they make the best effort to know us, but sometimes they show that they know
us better than ourselves. They can add layers of meaning behind our actions, our
inactions, our fears, our dreams, our hopes, our desires. And because we feel they get
us on such a deep level, deeper than we may even know ourselves, that's what earns
our trust and the ability to inspire and empower our next action.
So here's what's at play. And I really want you to start embodying these traits and
characteristics in your relationship with your audience. The first principle is contextual
awareness. You know this is a fancy way of saying they understand us. You know they
can reflect back our contacts and shine a mirror giving us just greater visibility on our
circumstance, both internal and external. And it's with this awareness, this newfound
gift of awareness that it gives us the ability to commit to new directions. You know in a
practical copywriting sense, clients get frustrated sometimes with me because I can't
evaluate how good an email is or isn't without context. Because the effectiveness, the
strength of an email, is completely relative to what's needed in that moment. You know
I need to know where did they come from? What did they see just prior to this? What
did they do or what didn't they do? Because sometimes inactions are more telling than
actions.
So in order to make people feel understood, you need to first prove that you know how
they got here. Validate their experience. Show them that you get everything that they
brought to this very moment. You know in a one on one selling scenario, a coach might
ask what's going on in your life that made you want to take this call? And when you
think about it it's the ultimate leaping off point, and it really plays into our next
principle, which is confidence. You know when you're able to confidently and accurately
reflect back your prospects context back to them, it instills confidence that first of all,
you get it. You get where they're coming from, you get what brought them here. You get
their resistance, you get their skepticism, you get their hope. You get it all. And because
of that, because you get it, because you get everything they are bringing to the table.
The outcomes you're promising now feel possible because it's grounded within their
current reality. It's not this lofty thing that feels unattainable. It's grounded in the
context of where they're currently at. You know so you root these aspirations in a clear
and complete understanding of the starting point. And that is extremely powerful.
The next thing is courage. You know in the presence of these people that we're talking
about, we feel this rush of new possibility. You know think about these people in your
life that we highlighted earlier in this lesson. You know that rush of excitement that you
feel. Places that you had felt stuck or constricted before, their influence and presence
makes us now feel expansive. Like it's safe to expand and go on that journey. There's
this renewed sense of possibility. You know they give us the courage to commit to a
more empowering vision, and it's very subtle yet very real. If you're really imagining this
person in your life who's presence makes you feel ready to expand, ready to grow in
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 5 --

The 5Cs of a Successful Conversion Coach (Completed 05/07/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 3 of 5
certain ways, that safety and courage that it instills, that's what we want to give to our
audience. Once again, at scale. 60,000 people in a single afternoon..
And likewise, they have the courage to ask us the tough questions and not let us off the
hook of that commitment. You know they challenge us. Just like at the beginning they
challenge our beliefs and views. They continue to challenge us deeply. You know
whenever we start to backslide into our beliefs or fall into old habits, patterns of
resistance; a good friend, a good coach, they will hold our feet to the fire and remind us
of our commitment.
So as a conversion coach, your main focus should be simply not letting your customers
fail. You know if they've committed to an outcome, in that high moment of courage
they've committed yes, I want to do this, yes I see that you've reflected back everything
about my prior contacts and now I believe that I can do this and I'm committed to doing
this. Part of your responsibility is not letting them fail. To challenge them not just
initially with beliefs in a new way, but challenge them throughout the funnel, especially
towards the close. To hold true to that commitment. Why? I mean, you know a skeptic
could say because we want more sales. But what we're really going for here is simply
care. Because you care. You don't just pay lip service to it. Just like how you feel when
someone legitimately cares about you. It has nothing to do with pushing a self-serving
agenda. You know most people could sniff that a mile away. You know they're not
asking you to change into a version of you that would be more convenient to them.
They're inviting you to change because they care deeply and intimately about your
growth and helping you stay true to the commitments you made to yourself. And it's
this authentic care that makes you feel so supported and courageous in their presence.
It's that level of care that I want you to develop with your prospects and your funnels.
You know Jay Abraham talks a lot about preeminence. You know which means always
having your audience's best interests in mind. You know and David [Olgilvy 00:11:09]
decades prior said the customer is not a moron, she's your wife. And further to that, you
know FOMO shouldn't just be this thing you ask your customer to feel in the minutes
before the cart closes. FOMO should be something you feel on behalf of them when you
sense their opportunity to overcome a pain or solve a problem is slipping. You know
these are the qualities that I really want you to cultivate as marketers, as educators, as
coaches and consultants. To really take these 5 C's and make them a part of every
interaction. Especially just caring about them. Caring about them and that really is what
earns you the right to hold them to those commitments. You know not in any kind of
self-serving way and not because you want the sale, but because you're just really
furthering their advancement, their growth, their overcoming of trials and tribulations.
And to me that is one of the finest gifts we can give as marketers, as educators, as
entrepreneurs. You know that's what brought many of us into the game to begin with;
to solve these problems, solve them at scale and get rewarded for it. So it's really just
honoring the essence of what we're doing as entrepreneurs.
And the final thing I want to talk about in this lesson is interruption versus permission
versus invitation marketing. You know many of us have heard the term interruption
marketing, which is essentially an ad that interrupts your prospect. You know and we've
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 5 --

The 5Cs of a Successful Conversion Coach (Completed 05/07/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 4 of 5
heard the term permission marketing which is when the prospect raises their hand and
says, "I want to hear from you." So this will usually come in the form of an email opt in.
But I want to challenge you right now to drop the term permission marketing because
it's just far too tinted for the level of work and the level of care we're trying to express
through our marketing.
So for the level of the work that we have ahead of us where we're literally taking our
prospect by the hand and coaching them into being the kind of person ready and
empowered to get the most out of your product, we really need to go one level deeper.
Permission is the moment they opt in. From then on it is invitation. You've been invited
into their life, and you have to assume until they say otherwise that they want you
there. You know until they state otherwise explicitly, you need to assume that not only
are you permitted, but you're welcomed and required to act in their best interest.
So in the next module you'll get introduced to a concept I call state checks. And this is
pretty much just reasserting that invitation and a few critical junctures in your funnel so
that you never have to feel bad or timid about being too challenging or emailing with
too much frequency. You know for now all you really need to know is that they asked
you to be there. They flashed the SOS signal in the air, they flashed that bat signal, and
now it's your responsibility to deliver on the value they're expecting from you. So you
don't need to be apologetic about emailing them. You don't need to be apologetic about
being confronting and challenging when the moment calls for it. Be like they're most
trusted friend who calls them out on their stuff. Who gives an objective view of what's
holding them back, and the encouragement and tools to get through it.
So that's it. That's what I want to leave you with here and before I head off and meet
you in module two, I'm just gonna give you a brief breakdown on why I hired my first
coach. You know a couple years ago, it's been about two and a half/three years ago now
that I hired my first coach. And it took me a while to unpack why I hired him. But looking
back retrospectively, all these factors were present. You know the decision was scary as
hell.
I was literally three days out of the stroke ward when I met him. I had quit my full time
job, had no income, had no idea how long it would take to feel normal again. You know I
remember my left hand was still typing faster than my right hand, which is kind of
problematic for a writer. And by all fair estimations I was in no place to invest in a coach.
Yet in that single hour he made me feel more heard and understood than anyone
before. He convinced me that my vision mattered. And he challenged me to boldly
commit to it. So even though the price tag at the time was enough to freak me out, the
courage he helped me find in that moment and the confidence that I could do it, was all
I needed to really sign on that dotted line on that day. So it wasn't predatory or sleazy or
anything like that, it was preeminent. And of the highest service. And the results of that
experience since then have been truly life changing. So once again, part of what we're
doing in a launch or online marketing funnel is trying to gradually level up those 10 X
conversion rates, that one on one collaborative selling can boast, but just doing it at
scale. And step one of doing that is being aware of these five traits of a successful coach
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

and consciously cultivating them. Building them into your communications with your
audience.
The rest of course will be all about how you can actually craft these elements into your
sales messaging in a very natural and authentic way that actually makes writing feel like
a joy and a gift. But with that, we're gonna take a break, then we're gonna meet back in
module two where you'll perhaps, for the first time really, and I mean really, really,
meet your customer. So I'll see you there.
